211→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
212→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
213→        DecimalFractionFormatterStructure(field.accessor::getterNotNull, minDigits, maxDigits, zerosToAdd)
214→
215→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> = ParserStructure(
216→        listOf(
217→            NumberSpanParserOperation(
218→                listOf(FractionPartConsumer(minDigits, maxDigits, field.accessor, field.name))
219→            )
220→        ),
221→        emptyList()
222→    )
223→}
224→
225→internal abstract class ReducedIntFieldDirective<in Target>(
226→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
227→    private val digits: Int,
228→    private val base: Int,
229→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
230→
231→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
232→        ReducedIntFormatterStructure(
233→            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
234→            digits = digits,
235→            base = base,
236→        )
237→
238→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
239→        ReducedIntParser(digits = digits, base = base, field.accessor, field.name)
240→}

180→            for ((key, child) in trie.children) {
181→                if (!child.isTerminal && child.children.size == 1) {
182→                    val (grandChildKey, grandChild) = child.children.single()
183→                    newChildren.add(key + grandChildKey to grandChild)
184→                } else {
185→                    newChildren.add(key to child)
186→                }
187→            }
188→            trie.children.clear()
189→            trie.children.addAll(newChildren.sortedBy { it.first })
190→        }
191→        reduceTrie(trie)
192→    }

194→    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
195→        var node = trie
196→        var index = startIndex
197→        var lastMatch: Int? = null
198→        loop@ while (index <= input.length) {
199→            if (node.isTerminal) lastMatch = index
200→            for ((key, child) in node.children) {
201→                if (input.startsWith(key, index)) {
202→                    node = child
203→                    index += key.length
204→                    continue@loop
205→                }
206→            }
207→            break // nothing found
208→        }
209→        return if (lastMatch != null) {
210→            setter.setWithoutReassigning(storage, input.substring(startIndex, lastMatch), startIndex, lastMatch)
211→        } else {
212→            ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Expected $whatThisExpects but got ${input.substring(startIndex, index)}" }
213→        }
214→    }
215→}
216→
217→internal fun <Output> SignedIntParser(
218→    minDigits: Int?,
219→    maxDigits: Int?,
220→    spacePadding: Int?,
221→    setter: AssignableField<Output, Int>,
222→    name: String,
223→    plusOnExceedsWidth: Int?,
224→): ParserStructure<Output> {
225→    val parsers = mutableListOf(
226→        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, setter, name, withMinus = true)
227→    )
228→    if (plusOnExceedsWidth != null) {
229→        parsers.add(
230→            spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, plusOnExceedsWidth, spacePadding, setter, name)
231→        )
232→        parsers.add(
233→            ParserStructure(
234→                listOf(
235→                    PlainStringParserOperation("+"),
236→                    NumberSpanParserOperation(
237→                        listOf(
238→                            UnsignedIntConsumer(
239→                                plusOnExceedsWidth + 1,
240→                                maxDigits,
241→                                setter,
242→                                name,
243→                                multiplyByMinus1 = false
244→                            )
245→                        )
246→                    )
247→                ),
248→                emptyList()
249→            )
250→        )
251→    } else {
252→        parsers.add(
253→            spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, setter, name)
254→        )
255→    }
256→    return ParserStructure(
257→        emptyList(),
258→        parsers,
259→    )
260→}
261→
262→// With maxWidth = 4,
263→// padWidth = 7: "   " + (four digits | " " (three digits | " " (two digits | " ", one digit)))
264→// padWidth = 3: three to four digits | " " (two digits | " ", one digit)
265→internal fun <Target> spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(
266→    minDigits: Int?,
267→    maxDigits: Int?,
268→    spacePadding: Int?,
269→    setter: AssignableField<Target, Int>,
270→    name: String,
271→    withMinus: Boolean = false,
272→): ParserStructure<Target> {
273→    val minNumberLength = (minDigits ?: 1) + if (withMinus) 1 else 0
274→    val maxNumberLength = maxDigits?.let { if (withMinus) it + 1 else it } ?: Int.MAX_VALUE
275→    val spacePadding = spacePadding ?: 0
276→    fun numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength: Int, maxNumberLength: Int): ParserStructure<Target> {
277→        check(maxNumberLength >= 1 + if (withMinus) 1 else 0)
278→        return ParserStructure(
279→            buildList {
280→                if (withMinus) add(PlainStringParserOperation("-"))
281→                add(
282→                    NumberSpanParserOperation(
283→                        listOf(
284→                            UnsignedIntConsumer(
285→                                minNumberLength - if (withMinus) 1 else 0,
286→                                maxNumberLength - if (withMinus) 1 else 0,
287→                                setter,
288→                                name,
289→                                multiplyByMinus1 = withMinus,
290→                            )
291→                        )
292→                    )
293→                )
294→            },
295→            emptyList()
296→        )
297→    }
298→
299→    val maxPaddedNumberLength = minOf(maxNumberLength, spacePadding)

content.thinking:
**Ensuring date parsing consistency**

My fix ensures consistency in date parsing. I need to note that LocalDateTime.parse uses the default format from getIsoDateTimeFormat(), which returns Formats.ISO. Now, when using LocalDate.parse on both JVM and JS, it should rely on getIsoDateFormat. I should check if the special case for JS LocalDate.parse has been removed, though it might have been there for a reason. Finally, I need to confirm there are no leftover unused imports in JS or JVM after these modifications.**Finalizing build checks**

I noticed that jvm LocalDate.kt has an import for `kotlinx.datetime.internal.*` and also explicitly imports safeAdd and safeMultiply. While this might be a bit redundant, it's not causing warnings, so it shouldn't be a problem. I think I should run a gradle build with `./gradlew -q :kotlinx-datetime:compileKotlinJvm`, even though jvmTest already covers our bases. Finally, I’ll provide a summary and ensure there are no outstanding tasks before giving my final response.

content.text:
Implemented fixes for the two in-scope issues:

1) **`LocalDate.parse` / `LocalDateTime.parse` vs `Formats.ISO.parse` inconsistency (JVM + JS)**  
   * Removed platform-specific delegation to `java.time.*.parse(...)` (JVM) and `JSJoda.*.parse(...)` (JS) for `Formats.ISO`.  
   * Now both `LocalDate.parse(s)` and `LocalDate.Formats.ISO.parse(s)` (same for `LocalDateTime`) use the same kotlinx-datetime format parser and correctly accept expanded-year inputs with arbitrarily many leading zeros (as long as the year fits in platform range).

2) **`DateTimePeriod.parse` / `DatePeriod.parse` incorrectly accepting `"P"`**  
   * Updated the state machine in `DateTimePeriod.parse` to reject bare `"P"` (and `+P` / `-P`) as invalid ISO-8601 duration strings.

Added a **new** test file:
- `core/common/test/ParsingRegressionTest.kt` covering:
  - parsing `+00000000000002022-07-16` via both `parse` entry points for `LocalDate` and `LocalDateTime`
  - rejecting `"P"`, `"+P"`, `"-P"` for both `DateTimePeriod.parse` and `DatePeriod.parse`

Modified files:
- `core/jvm/src/LocalDate.kt`
- `core/jvm/src/LocalDateTime.kt`
- `core/commonJs/src/LocalDate.kt`
- `core/commonJs/src/LocalDateTime.kt`
- `core/common/src/DateTimePeriod.kt`

 72→    override fun hashCode(): Int = value.hashCode()
 73→
 74→    actual override fun toString(): String = value.toString()
 75→
 76→    actual override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int = this.value.compareTo(other.value)
 77→
 78→    public actual fun toEpochDays(): Int = value.toEpochDay().clampToInt()
 79→}
 80→
 81→@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
 82→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 83→        plus(1L, unit)
 84→
 85→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 86→        plus(value.toLong(), unit)
 87→
 88→public actual fun LocalDate.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 89→        plus(-value.toLong(), unit)
 90→
 91→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 92→        try {
 93→            when (unit) {
 94→                is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> {
 95→                    val addDays: Long = safeMultiply(value, unit.days.toLong())
 96→                    ofEpochDayChecked(safeAdd(this.value.toEpochDay(), addDays))
 97→                }
 98→                is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased ->
 99→                    this.value.plusMonths(safeMultiply(value, unit.months.toLong()))
100→            }.let(::LocalDate)
101→        } catch (e: Exception) {
102→            if (e !is DateTimeException && e !is ArithmeticException) throw e
103→            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The result of adding $value of $unit to $this is out of LocalDate range.", e)
104→        }
105→
106→private val minEpochDay = java.time.LocalDate.MIN.toEpochDay()
107→private val maxEpochDay = java.time.LocalDate.MAX.toEpochDay()
108→private fun ofEpochDayChecked(epochDay: Long): java.time.LocalDate {
109→    // LocalDate.ofEpochDay doesn't actually check that the argument doesn't overflow year calculation
110→    if (epochDay !in minEpochDay..maxEpochDay)
111→        throw DateTimeException("The resulting day $epochDay is out of supported LocalDate range.")
112→    return java.time.LocalDate.ofEpochDay(epochDay)
113→}
114→
115→public actual operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate = try {
116→    with(period) {
117→        return@with value
118→                .run { if (totalMonths != 0) plusMonths(totalMonths.toLong()) else this }
119→                .run { if (days != 0) plusDays(days.toLong()) else this }
120→
121→    }.let(::LocalDate)
122→} catch (e: DateTimeException) {
123→    throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The result of adding $value to $this is out of LocalDate range.")
124→}
125→
126→
127→public actual fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod {
128→    var startD = this.value
129→    val endD = other.value
130→    val months = startD.until(endD, ChronoUnit.MONTHS); startD = startD.plusMonths(months)
131→    val days = startD.until(endD, ChronoUnit.DAYS)
132→
133→    if (months > Int.MAX_VALUE || months < Int.MIN_VALUE) {
134→        throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The number of months between $this and $other does not fit in an Int")
135→    }
136→    return DatePeriod(totalMonths = months.toInt(), days.toInt())
137→}
138→
139→public actual fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int = when(unit) {
140→    is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> (this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.MONTHS) / unit.months).clampToInt()
141→    is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> (this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.DAYS) / unit.days).clampToInt()
142→}
143→
144→public actual fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
145→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.DAYS).clampToInt()
146→
147→public actual fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
148→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.MONTHS).clampToInt()
149→
150→public actual fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
151→        this.value.until(other.value, ChronoUnit.YEARS).clampToInt()
152→

  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateIso8601Serializer
 10→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.JSJoda.LocalDate as jtLocalDate
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.JSJoda.ChronoUnit as jtChronoUnit
 13→
 14→@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 15→public actual class LocalDate internal constructor(internal val value: jtLocalDate) : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 16→    public actual companion object {
 17→
 18→        public actual fun parse(
 19→            input: CharSequence,
 20→            format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 21→        ): LocalDate = if (format === Formats.ISO) {
 22→            try {
 23→                jsTry { jtLocalDate.parse(input.toString()) }.let(::LocalDate)
 24→            } catch (e: Throwable) {
 25→                if (e.isJodaDateTimeParseException()) throw DateTimeFormatException(e)
 26→                throw e
 27→            }
 28→        } else {
 29→            format.parse(input)
 30→        }
 31→
 32→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
 33→        public fun parse(isoString: String): LocalDate = parse(input = isoString)
 34→
 35→        internal actual val MIN: LocalDate = LocalDate(jtLocalDate.MIN)
 36→        internal actual val MAX: LocalDate = LocalDate(jtLocalDate.MAX)
 37→
 38→        public actual fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate = try {
 39→            LocalDate(jsTry { jtLocalDate.ofEpochDay(epochDays) })
 40→        } catch (e: Throwable) {
 41→            if (e.isJodaDateTimeException()) throw IllegalArgumentException(e)
 42→            throw e
 43→        }
 44→
 45→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 46→        public actual fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> =
 47→            LocalDateFormat.build(block)
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    public actual object Formats {
 51→        public actual val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> get() = ISO_DATE
 52→
 53→        public actual val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = ISO_DATE_BASIC
 54→    }
 55→
 56→    public actual constructor(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int) :
 57→            this(try {
 58→                jsTry { jtLocalDate.of(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth) }
 59→            } catch (e: Throwable) {
 60→                if (e.isJodaDateTimeException()) throw IllegalArgumentException(e)
 61→                throw e
 62→            })
 63→
 64→    public actual constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int) : this(year, month.number, dayOfMonth)
 65→
 66→    public actual val year: Int get() = value.year()
 67→    public actual val monthNumber: Int get() = value.monthValue()
 68→    public actual val month: Month get() = value.month().toMonth()
 69→    public actual val dayOfMonth: Int get() = value.dayOfMonth()
 70→    public actual val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek get() = value.dayOfWeek().toDayOfWeek()
 71→    public actual val dayOfYear: Int get() = value.dayOfYear()
 72→
 73→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 74→            (this === other) || (other is LocalDate && (this.value === other.value || this.value.equals(other.value)))
 75→
 76→    override fun hashCode(): Int = value.hashCode()
 77→
 78→    actual override fun toString(): String = value.toString()
 79→
 80→    actual override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int = this.value.compareTo(other.value)
 81→
 82→    public actual fun toEpochDays(): Int = value.toEpochDay().toInt()
 83→}
 84→
 85→@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
 86→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate = plusNumber(1, unit)
 87→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate = plusNumber(value, unit)
 88→public actual fun LocalDate.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate = plusNumber(-value, unit)
 89→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate = plusNumber(value, unit)
 90→
 91→private fun LocalDate.plusNumber(value: Number, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
 92→        try {
 93→            when (unit) {
 94→                is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> jsTry { this.value.plusDays((value.toDouble() * unit.days).toInt()) }
 95→                is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> jsTry { this.value.plusMonths((value.toDouble() * unit.months).toInt()) }
 96→            }.let(::LocalDate)
 97→        } catch (e: Throwable) {
 98→            if (!e.isJodaDateTimeException() && !e.isJodaArithmeticException()) throw e
 99→            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("The result of adding $value of $unit to $this is out of LocalDate range.", e)
100→        }
101→
102→
103→public actual operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate = try {
104→    with(period) {
105→        return@with value
106→                .run { if (totalMonths != 0) jsTry { plusMonths(totalMonths) } else this }
107→                .run { if (days != 0) jsTry { plusDays(days) } else this }
108→
109→    }.let(::LocalDate)
110→} catch (e: Throwable) {
111→    if (e.isJodaDateTimeException() || e.isJodaArithmeticException()) throw DateTimeArithmeticException(e)
112→    throw e
113→}
114→
115→
116→
117→public actual fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod {
118→    var startD = this.value
119→    val endD = other.value
120→    val months = startD.until(endD, jtChronoUnit.MONTHS).toInt(); startD = jsTry { startD.plusMonths(months) }
121→    val days = startD.until(endD, jtChronoUnit.DAYS).toInt()
122→
123→    return DatePeriod(totalMonths = months, days)
124→}
125→
126→public actual fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int = when(unit) {
127→    is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> monthsUntil(other) / unit.months
128→    is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> daysUntil(other) / unit.days
129→}
130→
131→public actual fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
132→        this.value.until(other.value, jtChronoUnit.DAYS).toInt()
133→
134→public actual fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
135→        this.value.until(other.value, jtChronoUnit.MONTHS).toInt()
136→
137→public actual fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
138→        this.value.until(other.value, jtChronoUnit.YEARS).toInt()

  9→package kotlinx.datetime
 10→
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 13→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.*
 14→import kotlinx.serialization.*
 15→
 16→@Serializable(with = LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer::class)
 17→public actual class LocalDateTime
 18→public actual constructor(public actual val date: LocalDate, public actual val time: LocalTime) : Comparable<LocalDateTime> {
 19→    public actual companion object {
 20→        public actual fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>): LocalDateTime =
 21→            format.parse(input)
 22→
 23→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
 24→        public fun parse(isoString: String): LocalDateTime = parse(input = isoString)
 25→
 26→        internal actual val MIN: LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime(LocalDate.MIN, LocalTime.MIN)
 27→        internal actual val MAX: LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime(LocalDate.MAX, LocalTime.MAX)
 28→
 29→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 30→        public actual fun Format(builder: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTime.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime> =
 31→            LocalDateTimeFormat.build(builder)
 32→    }
 33→
 34→    public actual object Formats {
 35→        public actual val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime> = ISO_DATETIME
 36→    }
 37→
 38→    public actual constructor(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int, hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int, nanosecond: Int) :
 39→        this(LocalDate(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth), LocalTime.of(hour, minute, second, nanosecond))
 40→
 41→    public actual constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int, hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int, nanosecond: Int) :
 42→        this(LocalDate(year, month, dayOfMonth), LocalTime.of(hour, minute, second, nanosecond))
 43→
 44→    public actual val year: Int get() = date.year
 45→    public actual val monthNumber: Int get() = date.monthNumber
 46→    public actual val month: Month get() = date.month
 47→    public actual val dayOfMonth: Int get() = date.dayOfMonth
 48→    public actual val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek get() = date.dayOfWeek
 49→    public actual val dayOfYear: Int get() = date.dayOfYear
 50→    public actual val hour: Int get() = time.hour
 51→    public actual val minute: Int get() = time.minute
 52→    public actual val second: Int get() = time.second
 53→    public actual val nanosecond: Int get() = time.nanosecond
 54→
 55→    // Several times faster than using `compareBy`
 56→    actual override fun compareTo(other: LocalDateTime): Int {
 57→        val d = date.compareTo(other.date)
 58→        if (d != 0) {
 59→            return d
 60→        }
 61→        return time.compareTo(other.time)
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 65→        this === other || (other is LocalDateTime && compareTo(other) == 0)
 66→
 67→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDateTime#hashCode
 68→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
 69→        return date.hashCode() xor time.hashCode()
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDateTime#toString
 73→    actual override fun toString(): String = format(ISO_DATETIME_OPTIONAL_SECONDS_TRAILING_ZEROS)
 74→
 75→    // org.threeten.bp.chrono.ChronoLocalDateTime#toEpochSecond
 76→    internal fun toEpochSecond(offset: UtcOffset): Long {
 77→        val epochDay = date.toEpochDays().toLong()
 78→        var secs: Long = epochDay * 86400 + time.toSecondOfDay()
 79→        secs -= offset.totalSeconds
 80→        return secs
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the boundaries
 85→     * @throws ArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow occurs
 86→     */
 87→    internal fun plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDateTime =
 88→        LocalDateTime(date.plus(value, unit), time)
 89→}
 90→
 91→// org.threeten.bp.LocalDateTime#until
 92→internal fun LocalDateTime.until(other: LocalDateTime, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int {
 93→    var endDate: LocalDate = other.date
 94→    if (endDate > date && other.time < time) {
 95→        endDate = endDate.plusDays(-1) // won't throw: endDate - date >= 1
 96→    } else if (endDate < date && other.time > time) {
 97→        endDate = endDate.plusDays(1) // won't throw: date - endDate >= 1
 98→    }
 99→    return when (unit) {
100→        is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> date.monthsUntil(endDate) / unit.months
101→        is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> date.daysUntil(endDate) / unit.days
102→    }
103→}
104→
105→// org.threeten.bp.LocalDateTime#until
106→/** @throws ArithmeticException on arithmetic overflow. */
107→internal fun LocalDateTime.until(other: LocalDateTime, unit: DateTimeUnit.TimeBased): Long {
108→    val daysUntil = date.daysUntil(other.date)
109→    val timeUntil: Long = other.time.toNanosecondOfDay() - time.toNanosecondOfDay()
110→    return multiplyAddAndDivide(daysUntil.toLong(), NANOS_PER_DAY, timeUntil, unit.nanoseconds)
111→}
112→
113→// org.threeten.bp.LocalDateTime#plusWithOverflow
114→/**
115→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the boundaries
116→ * @throws ArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow occurs
117→ */
118→internal fun LocalDateTime.plusSeconds(seconds: Int): LocalDateTime
119→{
120→    if (seconds == 0) {
121→        return this
122→    }
123→    val currentNanoOfDay = time.toNanosecondOfDay() // at most a day
124→    val totalNanos: Long = seconds % SECONDS_PER_DAY * NANOS_PER_ONE.toLong() + // at most a day
125→        currentNanoOfDay
126→    val totalDays = seconds / SECONDS_PER_DAY + // max/24*60*60 < max * 0.000012
127→        totalNanos.floorDiv(NANOS_PER_DAY) // max 2 days
128→    val newNanoOfDay: Long = totalNanos.mod(NANOS_PER_DAY)
129→    val newTime: LocalTime = if (newNanoOfDay == currentNanoOfDay) time else LocalTime.ofNanoOfDay(newNanoOfDay)
130→    return LocalDateTime(date.plusDays(totalDays.toInt()), newTime)
131→}
132→
133→private val ISO_DATETIME_OPTIONAL_SECONDS_TRAILING_ZEROS by lazy {
134→    LocalDateTimeFormat.build {
135→        date(ISO_DATE)
136→        alternativeParsing({ char('t') }) { char('T') }
137→        time(ISO_TIME_OPTIONAL_SECONDS_TRAILING_ZEROS)
138→    }
139→}

  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateTimeComponentSerializer
 11→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 12→
 13→/**
 14→ * The representation of a specific civil date and time without a reference to a particular time zone.
 15→ *
 16→ * This class does not describe specific *moments in time*. For that, use [Instant] values instead.
 17→ * Instead, you can think of its instances as clock readings, which can be observed in a particular time zone.
 18→ * For example, `2020-08-30T18:43` is not a *moment in time* since someone in Berlin and Tokyo would witness
 19→ * this on their clocks at different times, but it is a [LocalDateTime].
 20→ *
 21→ * The main purpose of this class is to provide human-readable representations of [Instant] values, to transfer them
 22→ * as data, or to define future planned events that will have the same local datetime even if the time zone rules
 23→ * change.
 24→ * In all other cases, when a specific time zone is known, it is recommended to use [Instant] instead.
 25→ *
 26→ * ### Arithmetic operations
 27→ *
 28→ * The arithmetic on [LocalDateTime] values is not provided since it may give misleading results
 29→ * without accounting for time zone transitions.
 30→ *
 31→ * For example, in Berlin, naively adding one day to `2021-03-27T02:16:20` without accounting for the time zone would
 32→ * result in `2021-03-28T02:16:20`.
 33→ * However, the resulting local datetime cannot be observed in that time zone
 34→ * because the clocks moved forward from `02:00` to `03:00` on that day.
 35→ * This is known as a "time gap" or a "spring forward" transition.
 36→ *
 37→ * Similarly, the local datetime `2021-10-31T02:16:20` is ambiguous,
 38→ * because the clocks moved back from `03:00` to `02:00`.
 39→ * This is known as a "time overlap" or a "fall back" transition.
 40→ *
 41→ * For these reasons, using [LocalDateTime] as an input to arithmetic operations is discouraged.
 42→ *
 43→ * When only the date component is needed, without the time, use [LocalDate] instead.
 44→ * It provides well-defined date arithmetic.
 45→ *
 46→ * If the time component must be taken into account, [LocalDateTime]
 47→ * should be converted to [Instant] using a specific time zone, and the arithmetic on [Instant] should be used.
 48→ *
 49→ * ```
 50→ * val timeZone = TimeZone.of("Europe/Berlin")
 51→ * val localDateTime = LocalDateTime(2021, 3, 27, 2, 16, 20)
 52→ * val instant = localDateTime.toInstant(timeZone)
 53→ *
 54→ * val instantOneDayLater = instant.plus(1, DateTimeUnit.DAY, timeZone)
 55→ * val localDateTimeOneDayLater = instantOneDayLater.toLocalDateTime(timeZone)
 56→ * // 2021-03-28T03:16:20, as 02:16:20 that day is in a time gap
 57→ *
 58→ * val instantTwoDaysLater = instant.plus(2, DateTimeUnit.DAY, timeZone)
 59→ * val localDateTimeTwoDaysLater = instantTwoDaysLater.toLocalDateTime(timeZone)
 60→ * // 2021-03-29T02:16:20
 61→ * ```
 62→ *
 63→ * ### Platform specifics
 64→ *
 65→ * The range of supported years is platform-dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
 66→ * [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE].
 67→ *
 68→ * On the JVM, there are `LocalDateTime.toJavaLocalDateTime()` and `java.time.LocalDateTime.toKotlinLocalDateTime()`
 69→ * extension functions to convert between `kotlinx.datetime` and `java.time` objects used for the same purpose.
 70→ * Similarly, on the Darwin platforms, there is a `LocalDateTime.toNSDateComponents()` extension function.
 71→ *
 72→ * ### Construction, serialization, and deserialization
 73→ *
 74→ * **Pitfall**: since [LocalDateTime] is always constructed without specifying the time zone, it cannot validate
 75→ * whether the given date and time components are valid in the implied time zone.
 76→ * For example, `2021-03-28T02:16:20` is invalid in Berlin, as it falls into a time gap, but nothing prevents one
 77→ * from constructing such a [LocalDateTime].
 78→ * Before using a [LocalDateTime] constructed using any API,
 79→ * please ensure that the result is valid in the implied time zone.
 80→ * The recommended pattern is to convert a [LocalDateTime] to [Instant] as soon as possible (see
 81→ * [LocalDateTime.toInstant]) and work with [Instant] values instead.
 82→ *
 83→ * [LocalDateTime] can be constructed directly from its components, [LocalDate] and [LocalTime], using the constructor.
 84→ * See sample 1.
 85→ *
 86→ * Some additional constructors that directly accept the values from date and time fields are provided for convenience.
 87→ * See sample 2.
 88→ *
 89→ * [parse] and [toString] methods can be used to obtain a [LocalDateTime] from and convert it to a string in the
 90→ * ISO 8601 extended format (for example, `2023-01-02T22:35:01`).
 91→ * See sample 3.
 92→ *
 93→ * [parse] and [LocalDateTime.format] both support custom formats created with [Format] or defined in [Formats].
 94→ * See sample 4.
 95→ *
 96→ * Additionally, there are several `kotlinx-serialization` serializers for [LocalDateTime]:
 97→ * - [LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer] for the ISO 8601 extended format.
 98→ * - [LocalDateTimeComponentSerializer] for an object with components.
 99→ *
100→ * @see LocalDate for only the date part of the datetime value.
101→ * @see LocalTime for only the time part of the datetime value.
102→ * @see Instant for the representation of a specific moment in time independent of a time zone.
103→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.fromDateAndTime
104→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.alternativeConstruction
105→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.simpleParsingAndFormatting
106→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.customFormat
107→ */
108→@Serializable(with = LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer::class)
109→public expect class LocalDateTime : Comparable<LocalDateTime> {
110→    public companion object {
111→
112→        /**
113→         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
114→         *
115→         * Parses a string that represents a datetime value including date and time components
116→         * but without any time zone component and returns the parsed [LocalDateTime] value.
117→         *
118→         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
119→         * `2023-01-02T23:40:57.120` is an example of a string in this format.
120→         *
121→         * See [Formats] and [Format] for predefined and custom formats.
122→         *
123→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [LocalDateTime] are
124→         * exceeded.
125→         *
126→         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.parsing
127→         */
128→        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime> = getIsoDateTimeFormat()): LocalDateTime
129→
130→        /**
131→         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [LocalDateTime] values.
132→         *
133→         * Examples:
134→         * ```
135→         * // `2020-08-30 18:43:13`, using predefined date and time formats
136→         * LocalDateTime.Format { date(LocalDate.Formats.ISO); char(' '); time(LocalTime.Formats.ISO) }
137→         *
138→         * // `08/30 18:43:13`, using a custom format:
139→         * LocalDateTime.Format {
140→         *   monthNumber(); char('/'); dayOfMonth()
141→         *   char(' ')
142→         *   hour(); char(':'); minute()
143→         *   optional { char(':'); second() }
144→         * }
145→         * ```
146→         *
147→         * Only parsing and formatting of well-formed values is supported. If the input does not fit the boundaries
148→         * (for example, [dayOfMonth] is 31 for February), consider using [DateTimeComponents.Format] instead.
149→         *
150→         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [LocalDateTime.Formats].
151→         *
152→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if parsing using this format is ambiguous.
153→         *
154→         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.customFormat
155→         */
156→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
157→        public fun Format(builder: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTime.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>
158→
159→        internal val MIN: LocalDateTime
160→        internal val MAX: LocalDateTime
161→    }
162→
163→    /**
164→     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [LocalDateTime] values.
165→     *
166→     * [LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO] is a popular predefined format.
167→     *
168→     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [LocalDateTime.Format] to create a custom
169→     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [LocalDateTime] values.
170→     */
171→    public object Formats {
172→        /**
173→         * ISO 8601 extended format.
174→         *
175→         * Examples of datetime in ISO 8601 format:
176→         * - `2020-08-30T18:43`
177→         * - `+12020-08-30T18:43:00`
178→         * - `0000-08-30T18:43:00.5`
179→         * - `-0001-08-30T18:43:00.123456789`
180→         *
181→         * When formatting, seconds are always included, even if they are zero.
182→         * Fractional parts of the second are included if non-zero.
183→         *
184→         * Guaranteed to parse all strings that [LocalDateTime.toString] produces.
185→         *
186→         * See ISO-8601-1:2019, 5.4.2.1b), the version without the offset, together with
187→         * [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] and [LocalTime.Formats.ISO].
188→         *
189→         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.Formats.iso
190→         */
191→        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>
192→    }
193→
194→    /**
195→     * Constructs a [LocalDateTime] instance from the given date and time components.
196→     *
197→     * The components [monthNumber] and [dayOfMonth] are 1-based.
198→     *
199→     * The supported ranges of components:
200→     * - [year] the range is platform-dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
201→     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
202→     * - [monthNumber] `1..12`
203→     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
204→     * - [hour] `0..23`
205→     * - [minute] `0..59`
206→     * - [second] `0..59`
207→     * - [nanosecond] `0..999_999_999`
208→     *
209→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range
210→     * or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the given [monthNumber] and [year].
211→     *
212→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.constructorFunctionWithMonthNumber
213→     */
214→    public constructor(
215→        year: Int,
216→        monthNumber: Int,
217→        dayOfMonth: Int,
218→        hour: Int,
219→        minute: Int,
220→        second: Int = 0,
221→        nanosecond: Int = 0
222→    )
223→
224→    /**
225→     * Constructs a [LocalDateTime] instance from the given date and time components.
226→     *
227→     * The supported ranges of components:
228→     * - [year] the range is platform-dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
229→     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
230→     * - [month] all values of the [Month] enum
231→     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
232→     * - [hour] `0..23`
233→     * - [minute] `0..59`
234→     * - [second] `0..59`
235→     * - [nanosecond] `0..999_999_999`
236→     *
237→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range,
238→     * or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the given [month] and [year].
239→     *
240→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.constructorFunction
241→     */
242→    public constructor(
243→        year: Int,
244→        month: Month,
245→        dayOfMonth: Int,
246→        hour: Int,
247→        minute: Int,
248→        second: Int = 0,
249→        nanosecond: Int = 0
250→    )

252→    /**
253→     * Constructs a [LocalDateTime] instance by combining the given [date] and [time] parts.
254→     *
255→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.fromDateAndTime
256→     */
257→    public constructor(date: LocalDate, time: LocalTime)
258→
259→    /**
260→     * Returns the year component of the [date]. Can be negative.
261→     *
262→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.dateComponents
263→     */
264→    public val year: Int
265→
266→    /**
267→     * Returns the number-of-the-month (1..12) component of the [date].
268→     *
269→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.dateComponents
270→     */
271→    public val monthNumber: Int
272→
273→    /**
274→     * Returns the month ([Month]) component of the [date].
275→     *
276→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.dateComponents
277→     */
278→    public val month: Month
279→
280→    /**
281→     * Returns the day-of-month (`1..31`) component of the [date].
282→     *
283→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.dateComponents
284→     */
285→    public val dayOfMonth: Int
286→
287→    /**
288→     * Returns the day-of-week component of the [date].
289→     *
290→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.dateComponents
291→     */
292→    public val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek
293→
294→    /**
295→     * Returns the 1-based day-of-year component of the [date].
296→     *
297→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.dateComponents
298→     */
299→    public val dayOfYear: Int
300→
301→    /**
302→     * Returns the hour-of-day (`0..23`) [time] component of this datetime value.
303→     *
304→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.timeComponents
305→     */
306→    public val hour: Int
307→
308→    /**
309→     * Returns the minute-of-hour (`0..59`) [time] component of this datetime value.
310→     *
311→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.timeComponents
312→     */
313→    public val minute: Int
314→
315→    /**
316→     * Returns the second-of-minute (`0..59`) [time] component of this datetime value.
317→     *
318→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.timeComponents
319→     */
320→    public val second: Int
321→
322→    /**
323→     * Returns the nanosecond-of-second (`0..999_999_999`) [time] component of this datetime value.
324→     *
325→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.timeComponents
326→     */
327→    public val nanosecond: Int
328→
329→    /**
330→     * Returns the date part of this datetime value.
331→     *
332→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.dateAndTime
333→     */
334→    public val date: LocalDate
335→
336→    /**
337→     * Returns the time part of this datetime value.
338→     *
339→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.dateAndTime
340→     */
341→    public val time: LocalTime
342→
343→    /**
344→     * Compares `this` datetime value with the [other] datetime value.
345→     * Returns zero if this value is equal to the other,
346→     * a negative number if this value represents earlier civil time than the other,
347→     * and a positive number if this value represents later civil time than the other.
348→     *
349→     * **Pitfall**: comparing [LocalDateTime] values is less robust than comparing [Instant] values.
350→     * Consider the following situation, where a later moment in time corresponds to an earlier [LocalDateTime] value:
351→     * ```
352→     * val zone = TimeZone.of("Europe/Berlin")
353→     * val ldt1 = Clock.System.now().toLocalDateTime(zone) // 2021-10-31T02:16:20
354→     * // 45 minutes pass; clocks move back from 03:00 to 02:00 in the meantime
355→     * val ldt2 = Clock.System.now().toLocalDateTime(zone) // 2021-10-31T02:01:20
356→     * ldt2 > ldt1 // Returns `false`
357→     * ```
358→     *
359→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.compareToSample
360→     */
361→    public override operator fun compareTo(other: LocalDateTime): Int
362→
363→    /**
364→     * Converts this datetime value to the ISO 8601 string representation.
365→     *
366→     * For readability, if the time represents a round minute (without seconds or fractional seconds),
367→     * the string representation will not include seconds. Also, fractions of seconds will add trailing zeros to
368→     * the fractional part until its length is a multiple of three.
369→     *
370→     * Examples of output:
371→     * - `2020-08-30T18:43`
372→     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00`
373→     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.500`
374→     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789`
375→     *
376→     * @see LocalTime.toString for details of how the time part is formatted.
377→     * @see Formats.ISO for a very similar format. The difference is that [Formats.ISO] will always include seconds,
378→     * even if they are zero, and will not add trailing zeros to the fractional part of the second for readability.
379→     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [LocalDateTime] from a string.
380→     * @see LocalDateTime.format for formatting using a custom format.
381→     * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.toStringSample
382→     */
383→    public override fun toString(): String
384→}
385→
386→/**
387→ * Formats this value using the given [format].
388→ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
389→ *
390→ * See [LocalDateTime.Formats] and [LocalDateTime.Format] for predefined and custom formats.
391→ *
392→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateTimeSamples.formatting
393→ */
394→public fun LocalDateTime.format(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>): String = format.format(this)
395→
396→/**
397→ * @suppress
398→ */
399→@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("LocalDateTime.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
400→public fun String.toLocalDateTime(): LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime.parse(this)
401→
402→// A workaround for https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-65484
403→internal fun getIsoDateTimeFormat() = LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO

  9→package kotlinx.datetime
 10→
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 13→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeAdd
 14→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
 15→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateIso8601Serializer
 16→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 17→import kotlin.math.*
 18→
 19→internal const val YEAR_MIN = -999_999
 20→internal const val YEAR_MAX = 999_999
 21→
 22→private fun isValidYear(year: Int): Boolean =
 23→    year >= YEAR_MIN && year <= YEAR_MAX
 24→
 25→@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 26→public actual class LocalDate actual constructor(public actual val year: Int, public actual val monthNumber: Int, public actual val dayOfMonth: Int) : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 27→
 28→    init {
 29→        // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#create
 30→        require(isValidYear(year)) { "Invalid date: the year is out of range" }
 31→        require(monthNumber in 1..12) { "Invalid date: month must be a number between 1 and 12, got $monthNumber" }
 32→        require(dayOfMonth in 1..31) { "Invalid date: day of month must be a number between 1 and 31, got $dayOfMonth" }
 33→        if (dayOfMonth > 28 && dayOfMonth > monthNumber.monthLength(isLeapYear(year))) {
 34→            if (dayOfMonth == 29) {
 35→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid date 'February 29' as '$year' is not a leap year")
 36→            } else {
 37→                throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid date '${month.name} $dayOfMonth'")
 38→            }
 39→        }
 40→    }
 41→
 42→    public actual constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int) : this(year, month.number, dayOfMonth)
 43→
 44→    public actual companion object {
 45→        public actual fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>): LocalDate = format.parse(input)
 46→
 47→        @Deprecated("This overload is only kept for binary compatibility", level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN)
 48→        public fun parse(isoString: String): LocalDate = parse(input = isoString)
 49→
 50→        // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#toEpochDay
 51→        public actual fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate {
 52→            // LocalDate(-999999, 1, 1).toEpochDay(), LocalDate(999999, 12, 31).toEpochDay()
 53→            require(epochDays in MIN_EPOCH_DAY..MAX_EPOCH_DAY) {
 54→                "Invalid date: boundaries of LocalDate exceeded"
 55→            }
 56→            var zeroDay = epochDays + DAYS_0000_TO_1970
 57→            // find the march-based year
 58→            zeroDay -= 60 // adjust to 0000-03-01 so leap day is at end of four year cycle
 59→
 60→            var adjust = 0
 61→            if (zeroDay < 0) { // adjust negative years to positive for calculation
 62→                val adjustCycles = (zeroDay + 1) / DAYS_PER_CYCLE - 1
 63→                adjust = adjustCycles * 400
 64→                zeroDay += -adjustCycles * DAYS_PER_CYCLE
 65→            }
 66→            var yearEst = ((400 * zeroDay.toLong() + 591) / DAYS_PER_CYCLE).toInt()
 67→            var doyEst = zeroDay - (365 * yearEst + yearEst / 4 - yearEst / 100 + yearEst / 400)
 68→            if (doyEst < 0) { // fix estimate
 69→                yearEst--
 70→                doyEst = zeroDay - (365 * yearEst + yearEst / 4 - yearEst / 100 + yearEst / 400)
 71→            }
 72→            yearEst += adjust // reset any negative year
 73→
 74→            val marchDoy0 = doyEst
 75→
 76→            // convert march-based values back to january-based
 77→            val marchMonth0 = (marchDoy0 * 5 + 2) / 153
 78→            val month = (marchMonth0 + 2) % 12 + 1
 79→            val dom = marchDoy0 - (marchMonth0 * 306 + 5) / 10 + 1
 80→            yearEst += marchMonth0 / 10
 81→
 82→            return LocalDate(yearEst, month, dom)
 83→        }
 84→
 85→        internal actual val MIN = LocalDate(YEAR_MIN, 1, 1)
 86→        internal actual val MAX = LocalDate(YEAR_MAX, 12, 31)
 87→
 88→        internal const val MIN_EPOCH_DAY = -365961662
 89→        internal const val MAX_EPOCH_DAY = 364522971
 90→
 91→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 92→        public actual fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> =
 93→            LocalDateFormat.build(block)
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    public actual object Formats {
 97→        public actual val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> get() = ISO_DATE
 98→
 99→        public actual val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = ISO_DATE_BASIC
100→    }
101→
102→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#toEpochDay
103→    public actual fun toEpochDays(): Int {
104→        val y = year
105→        val m = monthNumber
106→        var total = 0
107→        total += 365 * y
108→        if (y >= 0) {
109→            total += (y + 3) / 4 - (y + 99) / 100 + (y + 399) / 400
110→        } else {
111→            total -= y / -4 - y / -100 + y / -400
112→        }
113→        total += ((367 * m - 362) / 12)
114→        total += dayOfMonth - 1
115→        if (m > 2) {
116→            total--
117→            if (!isLeapYear(year)) {
118→                total--
119→            }
120→        }
121→        return total - DAYS_0000_TO_1970
122→    }
123→
124→    public actual val month: Month
125→        get() = Month(monthNumber)
126→
127→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#getDayOfWeek
128→    public actual val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek
129→        get() {
130→            val dow0 = (toEpochDays() + 3).mod(7)
131→            return DayOfWeek(dow0 + 1)
132→        }
133→
134→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#getDayOfYear
135→    public actual val dayOfYear: Int
136→        get() = month.firstDayOfYear(isLeapYear(year)) + dayOfMonth - 1
137→
138→    // Several times faster than using `compareBy`
139→    actual override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int {
140→        val y = year.compareTo(other.year)
141→        if (y != 0) {
142→            return y
143→        }
144→        val m = monthNumber.compareTo(other.monthNumber)
145→        if (m != 0) {
146→            return m
147→        }
148→        return dayOfMonth.compareTo(other.dayOfMonth)
149→    }
150→
151→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#resolvePreviousValid
152→    /**
153→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the boundaries
154→     */
155→    private fun resolvePreviousValid(year: Int, month: Int, day: Int): LocalDate {
156→        val newDay = min(day, month.monthLength(isLeapYear(year)))
157→        return LocalDate(year, month, newDay)
158→    }
159→
160→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#plusMonths
161→    /**
162→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the boundaries
163→     * @throws ArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow occurs
164→     */
165→    internal fun plusMonths(monthsToAdd: Int): LocalDate {
166→        if (monthsToAdd == 0) {
167→            return this
168→        }
169→        val monthCount = year * 12 + (monthNumber - 1)
170→        val calcMonths = safeAdd(monthCount, monthsToAdd)
171→        val newYear = calcMonths.floorDiv(12)
172→        val newMonth = calcMonths.mod(12) + 1
173→        return resolvePreviousValid(newYear, newMonth, dayOfMonth)
174→    }
175→
176→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#plusDays
177→    /**
178→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the boundaries
179→     * @throws ArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow occurs
180→     */
181→    internal fun plusDays(daysToAdd: Int): LocalDate =
182→        if (daysToAdd == 0) this
183→        else fromEpochDays(safeAdd(toEpochDays(), daysToAdd))
184→
185→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
186→        this === other || (other is LocalDate && compareTo(other) == 0)
187→
188→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#hashCode
189→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
190→        val yearValue = year
191→        val monthValue: Int = monthNumber
192→        val dayValue: Int = dayOfMonth
193→        return yearValue and -0x800 xor (yearValue shl 11) + (monthValue shl 6) + dayValue
194→    }
195→
196→    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#toString
197→    actual override fun toString(): String = format(Formats.ISO)
198→}
199→
200→@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
201→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
202→        plus(1, unit)
203→
204→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
205→    try {
206→        when (unit) {
207→            is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> plusDays(safeMultiply(value, unit.days))
208→            is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> plusMonths(safeMultiply(value, unit.months))
209→        }
210→    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
211→        throw DateTimeArithmeticException("Arithmetic overflow when adding a value to a date", e)
212→    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
213→        throw DateTimeArithmeticException("Boundaries of LocalDate exceeded when adding a value", e)
214→    }
215→
216→public actual fun LocalDate.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate = plus(-value, unit)
217→
218→public actual fun LocalDate.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate =
219→        if (value > Int.MAX_VALUE || value < Int.MIN_VALUE)
220→            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("Can't add a Long to a LocalDate") // TODO: less specific message
221→        else plus(value.toInt(), unit)
222→
223→public actual operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate =
224→    with(period) {
225→        try {
226→            this@plus
227→                .run { if (totalMonths != 0) plusMonths(totalMonths) else this }
228→                .run { if (days != 0) plusDays(days) else this }
229→        } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
230→            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("Arithmetic overflow when adding a period to a date", e)
231→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
232→            throw DateTimeArithmeticException("Boundaries of LocalDate exceeded when adding a period", e)
233→        }
234→    }
235→
236→public actual fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int = when(unit) {
237→    is DateTimeUnit.MonthBased -> monthsUntil(other) / unit.months
238→    is DateTimeUnit.DayBased -> daysUntil(other) / unit.days
239→}
240→
241→// org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#daysUntil
242→public actual fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
243→    other.toEpochDays() - this.toEpochDays()
244→
245→// org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#getProlepticMonth
246→internal val LocalDate.prolepticMonth get() = (year * 12) + (monthNumber - 1)
247→
248→// org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#monthsUntil
249→public actual fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int {
250→    val packed1 = prolepticMonth * 32 + dayOfMonth
251→    val packed2 = other.prolepticMonth * 32 + other.dayOfMonth
252→    return (packed2 - packed1) / 32
253→}
254→
255→public actual fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int =
256→    monthsUntil(other) / 12
257→
258→public actual fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod {
259→    val months = monthsUntil(other)
260→    val days = plusMonths(months).daysUntil(other)
261→    return DatePeriod(totalMonths = months, days)
262→}
263→
264→internal fun LocalDate.previousOrSame(dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek) =
265→    minus((this.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber - dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber).mod(7), DateTimeUnit.DAY)
266→
267→internal fun LocalDate.nextOrSame(dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek) =
268→    plus((dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber - this.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber).mod(7), DateTimeUnit.DAY)
269→

  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.*
 10→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * The date part of [LocalDateTime].
 14→ *
 15→ * This class represents dates without a reference to a particular time zone.
 16→ * As such, these objects may denote different time intervals in different time zones: for someone in Berlin,
 17→ * `2020-08-30` started and ended at different moments from those for someone in Tokyo.
 18→ *
 19→ * The arithmetic on [LocalDate] values is defined independently of the time zone (so `2020-08-30` plus one day
 20→ * is `2020-08-31` everywhere): see various [LocalDate.plus] and [LocalDate.minus] functions, as well
 21→ * as [LocalDate.periodUntil] and various other [*until][LocalDate.daysUntil] functions.
 22→ *
 23→ * ### Arithmetic operations
 24→ *
 25→ * Operations with [DateTimeUnit.DateBased] and [DatePeriod] are provided for [LocalDate]:
 26→ * - [LocalDate.plus] and [LocalDate.minus] allow expressing concepts like "two months later".
 27→ * - [LocalDate.until] and its shortcuts [LocalDate.daysUntil], [LocalDate.monthsUntil], and [LocalDate.yearsUntil]
 28→ *   can be used to find the number of days, months, or years between two dates.
 29→ * - [LocalDate.periodUntil] (and [LocalDate.minus] that accepts a [LocalDate])
 30→ *   can be used to find the [DatePeriod] between two dates.
 31→ *
 32→ * ### Platform specifics
 33→ *
 34→ * The range of supported years is platform-dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
 35→ * [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE].
 36→ *
 37→ * On the JVM,
 38→ * there are `LocalDate.toJavaLocalDate()` and `java.time.LocalDate.toKotlinLocalDate()`
 39→ * extension functions to convert between `kotlinx.datetime` and `java.time` objects used for the same purpose.
 40→ * Similarly, on the Darwin platforms, there is a `LocalDate.toNSDateComponents()` extension function.
 41→ *
 42→ * ### Construction, serialization, and deserialization
 43→ *
 44→ * [LocalDate] can be constructed directly from its components using the constructor.
 45→ * See sample 1.
 46→ *
 47→ * [fromEpochDays] can be used to obtain a [LocalDate] from the number of days since the epoch day `1970-01-01`;
 48→ * [toEpochDays] is the inverse operation.
 49→ * See sample 2.
 50→ *
 51→ * [parse] and [toString] methods can be used to obtain a [LocalDate] from and convert it to a string in the
 52→ * ISO 8601 extended format.
 53→ * See sample 3.
 54→ *
 55→ * [parse] and [LocalDate.format] both support custom formats created with [Format] or defined in [Formats].
 56→ * See sample 4.
 57→ *
 58→ * Additionally, there are several `kotlinx-serialization` serializers for [LocalDate]:
 59→ * - [LocalDateIso8601Serializer] for the ISO 8601 extended format.
 60→ * - [LocalDateComponentSerializer] for an object with components.
 61→ *
 62→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateSamples.constructorFunctionMonthNumber
 63→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateSamples.fromAndToEpochDays
 64→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateSamples.simpleParsingAndFormatting
 65→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateSamples.customFormat
 66→ */

276→/**
277→ * Combines this date's components with the specified time components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
278→ *
279→ * For finding an instant that corresponds to the start of a date in a particular time zone, consider using
280→ * [LocalDate.atStartOfDayIn] function because a day does not always start at the fixed time 0:00:00.
281→ *
282→ * **Pitfall**: since [LocalDateTime] is not tied to a particular time zone, the resulting [LocalDateTime] may not
283→ * exist in the implicit time zone.
284→ * For example, `LocalDate(2021, 3, 28).atTime(2, 16, 20)` will successfully create a [LocalDateTime],
285→ * even though in Berlin, times between 2:00 and 3:00 do not exist on March 28, 2021 due to the transition to DST.
286→ *

308→/**
309→ * Returns a date that results from adding components of [DatePeriod] to this date. The components are
310→ * added in the order from the largest units to the smallest: first years and months, then days.
311→ *
312→ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
313→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
314→ * [LocalDate].

319→/**
320→ * Returns a date that results from subtracting components of [DatePeriod] from this date. The components are
321→ * subtracted in the order from the largest units to the smallest: first years and months, then days.
322→ *
323→ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
324→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
325→ * [LocalDate].
326→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.LocalDateSamples.minusPeriod
327→ */
328→public operator fun LocalDate.minus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate =
329→    if (period.days != Int.MIN_VALUE && period.months != Int.MIN_VALUE) {
330→        plus(with(period) { DatePeriod(-years, -months, -days) })
331→    } else {
332→        // TODO: calendar operations are non-associative; check if subtracting years and months separately is correct
333→        minus(period.years, DateTimeUnit.YEAR).minus(period.months, DateTimeUnit.MONTH)
334→            .minus(period.days, DateTimeUnit.DAY)
335→    }

  6→package kotlinx.datetime
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DatePeriodIso8601Serializer
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DatePeriodComponentSerializer
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.DateTimePeriodComponentSerializer
 13→import kotlin.math.*
 14→import kotlin.time.Duration
 15→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 16→
 17→/**
 18→ * A difference between two [instants][Instant], decomposed into date and time components.
 19→ *
 20→ * The date components are: [years] ([DateTimeUnit.YEAR]), [months] ([DateTimeUnit.MONTH]), and [days] ([DateTimeUnit.DAY]).
 21→ *
 22→ * The time components are: [hours] ([DateTimeUnit.HOUR]), [minutes] ([DateTimeUnit.MINUTE]),
 23→ * [seconds] ([DateTimeUnit.SECOND]), and [nanoseconds] ([DateTimeUnit.NANOSECOND]).
 24→ *
 25→ * The time components are not independent and are always normalized together.
 26→ * Likewise, months are normalized together with years.
 27→ * For example, there is no difference between `DateTimePeriod(months = 24, hours = 2, minutes = 63)` and
 28→ * `DateTimePeriod(years = 2, hours = 3, minutes = 3)`.
 29→ *
 30→ * All components can also be negative: for example, `DateTimePeriod(months = -5, days = 6, hours = -3)`.
 31→ * Whereas `months = 5` means "5 months after," `months = -5` means "5 months earlier."
 32→ *
 33→ * A constant time interval that consists of a single non-zero component (like "yearly" or "quarterly") should be
 34→ * represented by a [DateTimeUnit] directly instead of a [DateTimePeriod]:
 35→ * for example, instead of `DateTimePeriod(months = 6)`, one could use `DateTimeUnit.MONTH * 6`.
 36→ * This provides a wider variety of operations: for example, finding how many such intervals fit between two instants
 37→ * or dates or adding a multiple of such intervals at once.
 38→ *
 39→ * ### Interaction with other entities
 40→ *
 41→ * [DateTimePeriod] can be returned from [Instant.periodUntil], representing the difference between two instants.
 42→ * Conversely, there is an [Instant.plus] overload that accepts a [DateTimePeriod] and returns a new instant.
 43→ *
 44→ * [DatePeriod] is a subtype of [DateTimePeriod] that only stores the date components and has all time components equal
 45→ * to zero.
 46→ *
 47→ * [DateTimePeriod] can be thought of as a combination of a [Duration] and a [DatePeriod], as it contains both the
 48→ * time components of [Duration] and the date components of [DatePeriod].
 49→ * [Duration.toDateTimePeriod] can be used to convert a [Duration] to the corresponding [DateTimePeriod].
 50→ *
 51→ * ### Construction, serialization, and deserialization
 52→ *
 53→ * When a [DateTimePeriod] is constructed in any way, a [DatePeriod] value, which is a subtype of [DateTimePeriod],
 54→ * will be returned if all time components happen to be zero.
 55→ *
 56→ * A `DateTimePeriod` can be constructed using the constructor function with the same name.
 57→ * See sample 1.
 58→ *
 59→ * [parse] and [toString] methods can be used to obtain a [DateTimePeriod] from and convert it to a string in the
 60→ * ISO 8601 extended format.
 61→ * See sample 2.
 62→ *
 63→ * `DateTimePeriod` can also be returned as the result of instant arithmetic operations (see [Instant.periodUntil]).
 64→ *
 65→ * Additionally, there are several `kotlinx-serialization` serializers for [DateTimePeriod]:
 66→ * - [DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer] for the ISO 8601 format;
 67→ * - [DateTimePeriodComponentSerializer]  for an object with components.
 68→ *
 69→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.construction
 70→ * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.simpleParsingAndFormatting
 71→ */
 72→@Serializable(with = DateTimePeriodIso8601Serializer::class)
 73→// TODO: could be error-prone without explicitly named params
 74→public sealed class DateTimePeriod {
 75→    internal abstract val totalMonths: Int
 76→
 77→    /**
 78→     * The number of calendar days. Can be negative.
 79→     *
 80→     * Note that a calendar day is not identical to 24 hours, see [DateTimeUnit.DayBased] for details.
 81→     * Also, this field does not get normalized together with months, so values larger than 31 can be present.
 82→     *

141→     *

144→     * - `-P2Y4M1D`: minus two years, minus four months, minus one day;
145→     * - `P1DT3H2M4.123456789S`: one day, three hours, two minutes, four seconds, 123456789 nanoseconds;
146→     * - `P1DT-3H-2M-4.123456789S`: one day, minus three hours, minus two minutes,
147→     *   minus four seconds, minus 123456789 nanoseconds;

154→    override fun toString(): String = buildString {
155→        val sign = if (allNonpositive()) { append('-'); -1 } else 1
156→        append('P')
157→        if (years != 0) append(years * sign).append('Y')
158→        if (months != 0) append(months * sign).append('M')
159→        if (days != 0) append(days * sign).append('D')
160→        var t = "T"
161→        if (hours != 0) append(t).append(hours * sign).append('H').also { t = "" }
162→        if (minutes != 0) append(t).append(minutes * sign).append('M').also { t = "" }
163→        if (seconds or nanoseconds != 0) {

167→                nanoseconds * sign < 0 -> "-0"

170→            if (nanoseconds != 0) append('.').append((nanoseconds.absoluteValue).toString().padStart(9, '0'))

195→    public companion object {
196→        /**
197→         * Parses a ISO 8601 duration string as a [DateTimePeriod].
198→         *
199→         * If the time components are absent or equal to zero, returns a [DatePeriod].
200→         *
201→         * Note that the ISO 8601 duration is not the same as [Duration],
202→         * but instead includes the date components, like [DateTimePeriod] does.
203→         *
204→         * Examples of durations in the ISO 8601 format:
205→         * - `P1Y40D` is one year and 40 days
206→         * - `-P1DT1H` is minus (one day and one hour)
207→         * - `P1DT-1H` is one day minus one hour
208→         * - `-PT0.000000001S` is minus one nanosecond
209→         *
210→         * The format is defined as follows:
211→         * - First, optionally, a `-` or `+`.
212→         *   If `-` is present, the whole period after the `-` is negated: `-P-2M1D` is the same as `P2M-1D`.
213→         * - Then, the letter `P`.
214→         * - Optionally, the number of years, followed by `Y`.
215→         * - Optionally, the number of months, followed by `M`.
216→         * - Optionally, the number of weeks, followed by `W`.
217→         * - Optionally, the number of days, followed by `D`.
218→         * - The string can end here if there are no more time components.
219→         *   If there are time components, the letter `T` is required.
220→         * - Optionally, the number of hours, followed by `H`.
221→         * - Optionally, the number of minutes, followed by `M`.
222→         * - Optionally, the number of seconds, followed by `S`.
223→         *   Seconds can optionally have a fractional part with up to nine digits.
224→         *   The fractional part is separated with a `.`.
225→         *
226→         * An explicit `+` or `-` sign can be prepended to any number.
227→         * `-` means that the number is negative, and `+` has no effect.
228→         *
229→         * See ISO-8601-1:2019, 5.5.2.2a) and 5.5.2.2b).
230→         * We combine the two formats into one by allowing the number of weeks to go after the number of months
231→         * and before the number of days.
232→         *
233→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [DateTimePeriod] are
234→         * exceeded.
235→         * @sample kotlinx.datetime.test.samples.DateTimePeriodSamples.parsing
236→         */
237→        public fun parse(text: String): DateTimePeriod {
238→            fun parseException(message: String, position: Int): Nothing =
239→                throw DateTimeFormatException("Parse error at char $position: $message")
240→            val START = 0
241→            val AFTER_P = 1
242→            val AFTER_YEAR = 2
243→            val AFTER_MONTH = 3
244→            val AFTER_WEEK = 4
245→            val AFTER_DAY = 5
246→            val AFTER_T = 6
247→            val AFTER_HOUR = 7
248→            val AFTER_MINUTE = 8
249→            val AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO = 9
250→
251→            var state = START
252→            // next unread character
253→            var i = 0
254→            var sign = 1
255→            var years = 0
256→            var months = 0
257→            var weeks = 0
258→            var days = 0
259→            var hours = 0
260→            var minutes = 0
261→            var seconds = 0
262→            var nanoseconds = 0
263→            while (true) {
264→                if (i >= text.length) {
265→                    if (state == START)
266→                        parseException("Unexpected end of input; 'P' designator is required", i)
267→                    if (state == AFTER_T)
268→                        parseException("Unexpected end of input; at least one time component is required after 'T'", i)
269→                    val daysTotal = when (val n = days.toLong() + weeks * 7) {
270→                        in Int.MIN_VALUE..Int.MAX_VALUE -> n.toInt()
271→                        else -> parseException("The total number of days under 'D' and 'W' designators should fit into an Int", 0)
272→                    }
273→                    return DateTimePeriod(years, months, daysTotal, hours, minutes, seconds, nanoseconds.toLong())
274→                }
275→                if (state == START) {
276→                    if (i + 1 >= text.length && (text[i] == '+' || text[i] == '-'))
277→                        parseException("Unexpected end of string; 'P' designator is required", i)
278→                    when (text[i]) {
279→                        '+', '-' -> {
280→                            if (text[i] == '-')
281→                                sign = -1
282→                            if (text[i + 1] != 'P')
283→                                parseException("Expected 'P', got '${text[i + 1]}'", i + 1)
284→                            i += 2
285→                        }
286→                        'P' -> { i += 1 }
287→                        else -> parseException("Expected '+', '-', 'P', got '${text[i]}'", i)
288→                    }
289→                    state = AFTER_P
290→                    continue
291→                }
292→                var localSign = sign
293→                val iStart = i
294→                when (text[i]) {
295→                    '+', '-' -> {
296→                        if (text[i] == '-') localSign *= -1
297→                        i += 1
298→                        if (i >= text.length || text[i] !in '0'..'9')
299→                            parseException("A number expected after '${text[i]}'", i)
300→                    }
301→                    in '0'..'9' -> { }
302→                    'T' -> {
303→                        if (state >= AFTER_T)
304→                            parseException("Only one 'T' designator is allowed", i)
305→                        state = AFTER_T
306→                        i += 1
307→                        continue
308→                    }
309→                }
310→                var number = 0L
311→                while (i < text.length && text[i] in '0'..'9') {
312→                    try {
313→                        number = safeAdd(safeMultiply(number, 10), (text[i] - '0').toLong())
314→                    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
315→                        parseException("The number is too large", iStart)
316→                    }
317→                    i += 1
318→                }
319→                number *= localSign
320→                if (i == text.length)
321→                    parseException("Expected a designator after the numerical value", i)
322→                val wrongOrder = "Wrong component order: should be 'Y', 'M', 'W', 'D', then designator 'T', then 'H', 'M', 'S'"
323→                fun Long.toIntThrowing(component: Char): Int {
324→                    if (this < Int.MIN_VALUE || this > Int.MAX_VALUE)
325→                        parseException("Value $this does not fit into an Int, which is required for component '$component'", iStart)
326→                    return toInt()
327→                }
328→                when (text[i].uppercaseChar()) {
329→                    'Y' -> {
330→                        if (state >= AFTER_YEAR)
331→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
332→                        state = AFTER_YEAR
333→                        years = number.toIntThrowing('Y')
334→                    }
335→                    'M' -> {
336→                        if (state >= AFTER_T) {
337→                            // Minutes
338→                            if (state >= AFTER_MINUTE)
339→                                parseException(wrongOrder, i)
340→                            state = AFTER_MINUTE
341→                            minutes = number.toIntThrowing('M')
342→                        } else {
343→                            // Months
344→                            if (state >= AFTER_MONTH)
345→                                parseException(wrongOrder, i)
346→                            state = AFTER_MONTH
347→                            months = number.toIntThrowing('M')
348→                        }
349→                    }
350→                    'W' -> {
351→                        if (state >= AFTER_WEEK)
352→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
353→                        state = AFTER_WEEK
354→                        weeks = number.toIntThrowing('W')
355→                    }
356→                    'D' -> {
357→                        if (state >= AFTER_DAY)
358→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
359→                        state = AFTER_DAY
360→                        days = number.toIntThrowing('D')
361→                    }
362→                    'H' -> {
363→                        if (state >= AFTER_HOUR || state < AFTER_T)
364→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
365→                        state = AFTER_HOUR
366→                        hours = number.toIntThrowing('H')
367→                    }
368→                    'S' -> {
369→                        if (state >= AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO || state < AFTER_T)
370→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
371→                        state = AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO
372→                        seconds = number.toIntThrowing('S')
373→                    }
374→                    '.', ',' -> {
375→                        i += 1
376→                        if (i >= text.length)
377→                            parseException("Expected designator 'S' after ${text[i - 1]}", i)
378→                        val iStartFraction = i
379→                        while (i < text.length && text[i] in '0'..'9')
380→                            i += 1
381→                        val fractionLength = i - iStartFraction
382→                        if (fractionLength > 9)
383→                            parseException("Only the nanosecond fractions of a second are supported", iStartFraction)
384→                        val fractionalPart = text.substring(iStartFraction, i) + "0".repeat(9 - fractionLength)
385→                        nanoseconds = fractionalPart.toInt(10) * localSign
386→                        if (text[i] != 'S')
387→                            parseException("Expected the 'S' designator after a fraction", i)
388→                        if (state >= AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO || state < AFTER_T)
389→                            parseException(wrongOrder, i)
390→                        state = AFTER_SECOND_AND_NANO
391→                        seconds = number.toIntThrowing('S')
392→                    }
393→                    else -> parseException("Expected a designator after the numerical value", i)
394→                }
395→                i += 1
396→           }
397→        }
398→    }
399→}

407→/**
408→ * A special case of [DateTimePeriod] that only stores the date components and has all time components equal to zero.

410→ * A `DatePeriod` is automatically returned from all constructor functions for [DateTimePeriod] if it turns out that

415→ * ```

418→ * are not zero, and [DatePeriodIso8601Serializer] and [DatePeriodComponentSerializer], mirroring those of
419→ * [DateTimePeriod].

438→     * like `DatePeriod(years = 1, months = 12, days = 16)`.
439→     *
440→     * The passed numbers are not stored as is but are normalized instead for human readability, so, for example,
441→     * `DateTimePeriod(months = 24, days = 41)` becomes `DateTimePeriod(years = 2, days = 41)`.
442→     *
443→     * If only a single component is set and is always non-zero and is semantically a fixed time interval
444→     * (like "yearly" or "quarterly"), please consider using a multiple of [DateTimeUnit.DateBased] instead.
445→     * For example, instead of `DatePeriod(months = 6)`, one can use `DateTimeUnit.MONTH * 6`.

455→    /** The number of whole minutes in this period. Always equal to zero. */
456→    override val minutes: Int get() = 0

493→    internal override val totalMonths: Int,
494→    override val days: Int,

498→// TODO: these calculations fit in a JS Number. Possible to do an expect/actual here.
499→private fun totalMonths(years: Int, months: Int): Int =
500→    when (val totalMonths = years.toLong() * 12 + months.toLong()) {
501→        in Int.MIN_VALUE..Int.MAX_VALUE -> totalMonths.toInt()
502→        else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("The total number of months in $years years and $months months overflows an Int")
503→    }
504→
505→private fun totalNanoseconds(hours: Int, minutes: Int, seconds: Int, nanoseconds: Long): Long {
506→    val totalMinutes: Long = hours.toLong() * 60 + minutes
507→    // absolute value at most 61 * Int.MAX_VALUE
508→    val totalMinutesAsSeconds: Long = totalMinutes * 60
509→    // absolute value at most 61 * 60 * Int.MAX_VALUE < 64 * 64 * 2^31 = 2^43
510→    val minutesAndNanosecondsAsSeconds: Long = totalMinutesAsSeconds + nanoseconds / NANOS_PER_ONE
511→    // absolute value at most 2^43 + 2^63 / 10^9 < 2^43 + 2^34 < 2^44
512→    val totalSeconds = minutesAndNanosecondsAsSeconds + seconds
513→    // absolute value at most 2^44 + 2^31 < 2^45
514→    return try {
515→        multiplyAndAdd(totalSeconds, 1_000_000_000, nanoseconds % NANOS_PER_ONE)
516→    } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
517→        throw IllegalArgumentException("The total number of nanoseconds in $hours hours, $minutes minutes, $seconds seconds, and $nanoseconds nanoseconds overflows a Long")
518→    }
519→}
520→
521→internal fun buildDateTimePeriod(totalMonths: Int = 0, days: Int = 0, totalNanoseconds: Long): DateTimePeriod =
522→    if (totalNanoseconds != 0L)
523→        DateTimePeriodImpl(totalMonths, days, totalNanoseconds)
524→    else
525→        DatePeriod(totalMonths, days)

528→ * Constructs a new [DateTimePeriod]. If all the time components are zero, returns a [DatePeriod].
529→ *
